summary: runtimedev-link ships a persistent RAT disguised as telemetry
details: The package presents itself as 'Pure Node.js telemetry for RuntimeDev platform' but implements a full remote-access trojan against the installer. On activation the agent enters a polling loop against a configurable HTTP endpoint (/api/telemetry/poll-command) and passes any 'command' string returned by that server to child_process.execSync under /bin/sh or cmd.exe, streaming stdout/stderr back to /api/telemetry/command-result — an operator-controlled shell on the installer host. A second C2 branch (/api/telemetry/upload-download) accepts an arbitrary server-chosen path, ZIPs it (up to 50MB / 5000 files) and uploads the base64-encoded archive; /api/telemetry/directory-scan-result returns directory trees for any requested root, giving the operator unrestricted read of the installer's filesystem. A persistent 45–120s telemetry loop unconditionally POSTs hostname, username, OS info, the public IP fetched from api.ipify.org, and a walk of $HOME to /api/telemetry/report, and includes an inventory of Chrome, Edge, Brave, and Chromium extensions collected from the installer's browser user-data directories on Windows/macOS/Linux — data the package did not create. The `install` subcommand establishes hidden multi-platform persistence: a systemd --user unit with linger, a cron @reboot entry, an XDG autostart.desktop file, a macOS launchd plist (com.runtimedev.link, RunAtLoad + KeepAlive), and a Windows Scheduled Task registered with <Hidden>true</Hidden> launched via wscript.exe //B (CREATE_NO_WINDOW) — designed to survive reboot and stay off-screen. To decouple execution from the installer's Node, the agent downloads Node.js v22.22.0 into ~/.local/share/runtimedev-link/runtime, extracts it via tar -xJf, chmods bin/node, and relaunches itself under this private runtime.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
